name: github-issues
description: GitHub issues management assistant for Logseq Template Graph. Analyzes issues, triages with labels, plans implementations, generates responses, creates PRs, and manages issue lifecycle. Use when handling bug reports, feature requests, questions, or coordinating development through GitHub issues.
capabilities: Analyze and validate issues against codebase and git history
Apply appropriate labels based on type, priority, status, and scope
Generate responses for bugs, features, questions, and documentation requests
Identify duplicates, dependencies, and related issues
Create implementation plans and track issue lifecycle
Coordinate with other skills for Schema.org research and commits
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This skill is pure documentation containing only markdown workflows, templates, and CLI command examples for GitHub issue management. All 262 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The flagged patterns are bash documentation examples (not executable Ruby/shell code), standard git/gh CLI commands for legitimate operations, and harmless documentation URLs. No executable code, scripts, or malicious operations exist.
valueStatement: Manually triaging GitHub issues takes significant time and can lead to inconsistent responses. This skill automates issue analysis, validation, labeling, and response generation using structured workflows and best practices.
userTitle: Manage GitHub issues with AI assistance
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) installed and authenticated
Limited to Logseq Template Graph project context
Cannot access private repositories without gh authentication

useCases.title: Automated issue triage
useCases.description: Automatically validate bug reports, identify duplicates, and apply labels to new issues within 48 hours

useCases.title: Feature request handling
useCases.description: Generate implementation plans, research Schema.org alignment, and coordinate dependency tracking

useCases.title: Workflow coordination
useCases.description: Track issue progress, link PRs to issues, and manage release announcements

promptTemplates.title: Triage new issue
promptTemplates.scenario: A new issue was just opened
promptTemplates.prompt: Triage GitHub issue #123. Validate if the bug exists, search for duplicates, check git history, and provide a response with appropriate labels.

promptTemplates.title: Analyze feature request
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze feature request #456. Check if feature already exists, find related issues, research Schema.org alignment, and create an implementation plan.

promptTemplates.title: Close duplicate issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Issue #789 is a duplicate of #456. Close it with a helpful response linking to the original and encourage adding context there.

promptTemplates.title: Generate release notes
promptTemplates.prompt: Issue #321 is now fixed in PR #325. Update the issue with release notes, close it, and summarize the changes for users.

bestPractices: Always validate issues against codebase before responding - never assume reporter is correct
Search open and closed issues to identify duplicates and related work
Apply labels consistently using the taxonomy: type, priority, status, scope, and special labels
antiPatterns: Responding without validation - always check git history and codebase first
Leaving issues unlabeled - apply appropriate labels immediately
Ignoring duplicates - always search for related issues before responding

faq.question: What tools are required for this skill?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI (gh) must be installed and authenticated. Logseq Template Graph project context required.

faq.question: Can this skill work with private repositories?
faq.answer: Yes, if gh is authenticated with appropriate permissions for the private repository.

faq.question: What issue types are supported?
faq.answer: Bugs, features, questions, documentation requests, and refactoring tasks with templates for each.

faq.question: How does duplicate detection work?
faq.answer: Searches issue titles, bodies, and git history for keywords and related concepts using gh and grep.

faq.question: Can this skill create branches and commits?
faq.answer: Yes, provides git commands for creating branches, commits, and PRs linking to issues.

faq.question: How does this compare to GitHub Actions automation?
faq.answer: This skill provides AI-powered analysis and responses. GitHub Actions handles automation. Best used together.
